description: Quiz testing comprehension of Things Fall Apart, its title justification, and cultural conflict themes in Chinua Achebe's postcolonial novel.

questions.question: From which W.B. Yeats poem is the title 'Things Fall Apart' taken?
questions.explanation: The content explicitly states that the title is taken from a line in W.B. Yeats' poem 'The Second Coming' (1919): 'Things fall apart; the centre cannot hold.'

questions.question: In which region of Nigeria is the novel Things Fall Apart primarily set?
questions.explanation: The content states that Things Fall Apart is set in Umuofia, a cluster of nine Igbo villages in southeastern Nigeria, during the late 1890s.

questions.question: Approximately how many languages has Things Fall Apart been translated into?
questions.explanation: According to the content, Things Fall Apart has been translated into more than 50 languages and has sold over 20 million copies worldwide.

questions.question: What was Chinua Achebe's primary goal as a writer?
options: To entertain European audiences with African stories
To present African society from an African perspective and counter distorted colonial images
To document British colonial achievements in Africa
To promote Christian missionary work in Nigeria
questions.correctAnswer: To present African society from an African perspective and counter distorted colonial images
questions.explanation: The content explicitly states that Achebe's primary goal was 'to present African society from an African perspective, countering the distorted images produced by European colonial literature.'

questions.question: Things Fall Apart is considered a landmark of postcolonial literature because it was one of the first major works to tell the story of colonialism from the colonised perspective rather than the coloniser's perspective.
questions.explanation: The content states: 'The novel is considered a landmark of postcolonial literature because it was one of the first major works by an African writer to tell the story of colonialism from the colonised perspective, not the coloniser's.'

questions.question: Chinua Achebe wrote the essay 'An Image of Africa' to praise Joseph Conrad's portrayal of Africa in Heart of Darkness.
questions.explanation: The content states that in his essay 'An Image of Africa' (1975), Achebe 'challenged the racist portrayal of Africa in Joseph Conrad's Heart of Darkness,' not praised it.
